summary: Clean-room warp-per-(token,head) fused RMSNorm(fp32)+RoPE in-place, 64-bit vectorized bf16 load/store; production fast path (head_dim=128, rope_dim=128, is_neox=False, bf16, contiguous, equal Q/K heads, int32/int64 positions) + SGLang baseline fallback.
correctness: 18/18 (10 production incl eps=1e-5, int32, 4 fallback, arange) vs SGLang split oracle under ATOL=8e-2/RTOL=1e-2 + fp32 dynamic bound

ncu.active_bound: L2/compute/latency (NOT DRAM-bound); uncoalesced cos/sin gather 28% excessive sectors, Est. 24.66%

source_lineage: SGLang fused_qknorm_rope_warp (python/sglang/jit_kernel/csrc/diffusion/qknorm_rope.cuh @ 0b65588c1): warp-per-head mapping, fp32 RMS, interleaved RoPE, cos_sin layout — algorithm ported clean-room via torch cpp_extension (no SGLang-internal headers)

summary: v1 + NCU-guided fix: coalesced cos/sin via one float2 __ldg per lane (was strided scalar __ldg, the 28%-excessive-sector source) and FMA in the RMS reduction.

ncu.active_bound: L2/compute/latency-bound; candidate uncoalesced flag removed (baseline still carries 28%/Est.23.75%); kernels near parity in GPU time (60.1 vs 59.0 us)
ncu.residual_headroom: ~10% NCU Est. (residual uncoalesced load + FP32 fusion)

next_directions: 128-bit (16B) vectorized load/store via 2-heads-per-warp to cut instruction/issue count on large shapes
cold-cache (L2-flush) benchmark variant to characterize the true DRAM-bound roofline vs the L2-resident repeated-launch numbers
source_lineage: NCU rule engine (Uncoalesced Global Accesses) directed the float2 cos/sin coalescing fix

summary: v2 kernel math unchanged + Codex-review (task9) dispatcher hardening: _supported now checks aux-tensor device equality, positions contiguity, and cos_sin_cache rank; native TORCH_CHECKs mirror the gate (k dtype, positions cuda/contiguous, cache 2-D) for direct-extension-call safety; self-reference recursion guard in the fallback.

benchmark.note: large unchanged vs v2 (same kernel math); tiny dropped 1.187x->1.060x from ~1us added Python gate checks on the launch/dispatch-bound tiny shapes (safety > artificial dispatch latency; tiny shapes are dispatch-bound by design)

ncu: unchanged vs cand_v2 (kernel compute path identical)
addresses: Codex task9 REQUIRED_CHANGES (device/contiguity/rank gate, native check mirror); recursion caveat (self-reference guard)

summary: 2-heads-per-warp, 128-bit (float4) vectorized load/store: each lane owns 8 bf16 of one head, 16 lanes/head, half-warp (16-lane) RMS reduction; cos/sin loaded as float4 per lane (the 2 heads share a token). Raises memory-level parallelism on the latency-bound large shapes.
correctness: 21/21 (half-warp reduction + 128-bit packing + RoPE indexing validated vs oracle + fp32 dynamic bound)

benchmark.method: one invocation per CUDA-event sample, pristine Q/K reset+sync before each timed sample; GPU idle before=0%/4MB and after-idle=0% (settled) recorded in benchmark.csv
benchmark.note: single-call timing has ~+/-0.02 run-to-run variance on the geomeans; candidate beats baseline on every shape across runs. Tiny-bucket absolute latency includes per-sample reset+sync overhead (equal for both impls) so the ratio is the meaningful metric.

roofline_cold.note: v3 lifts cold achieved DRAM BW ~30%->~40% of 8 TB/s peak; still latency-bound (in-place RMW + small per-token work) but clearly ahead of baseline

ncu.active_bound: latency-bound with improved MLP; duration 60.1->50.1us vs v2; no uncoalesced flag

decision: PROMOTE — beats baseline on every shape incl the wall-clock-dominant large bucket
source_lineage: cold-cache roofline (BL-20260531-l2-resident-bench) diagnosed the low-MLP latency bound; 128-bit + 2-heads-per-warp is the MLP lever
